gsm8k.note: d2Cache model is 15x slower than HF model; speedup is internal only
d2Cache model is 15x slower than HF model; speedup is internal only

decision.detail: d2Cache PrefixCache achieves 6.33x speedup relative to d2Cache's own model (3.85 TPS -> 24.34 TPS), but d2Cache's model (3.85 TPS without cache) is 15.2x SLOWER than the HF baseline (58.5 TPS). Net effect: PrefixCache TPS (24.34) is still 0.42x the HF baseline. Root cause: d2Cache model uses eager attention (required for attention weight output) and additional context manager overhead per layer. On RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell, these costs dwarf the cache savings. Entropy-based CHR measurements show 93-99% cache hit rate across thresholds, giving theoretical speedup of 2.1-2.5x if kernel-level sparse attention is implemented without d2Cache's framework overhead. Report M1 with measured CHR and theoretical speedup, labeled as 'projected'.
decision.pass_criteria_note: TPS 1.5x threshold is vs HF baseline (58.5 TPS), not d2Cache internal model
decision.recommendation_for_m1_pareto: Use simplified entropy-based cache implementation from iter_001 with measured CHR data from this pilot. Report speedup as theoretical/projected based on CHR * attention_fraction savings. For actual TPS, rely on the HF model baseline. The gap between theoretical and actual speedup (d2Cache overhead) is itself a finding.

vram.gpu_name: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
